body:
9.40 THESIS -- PART 3 of 3 (Sections 4-5). Parts 1-2 still queued locally, will sync once push succeeds.

=== SECTION 4: WHAT 9.40 CHANGES AND WHY ===

The honest case, not the aspirational one: 9.40 should NOT promise a bigger edge. No amount of code review invents alpha that isn't in the market data. What 9.40 CAN honestly deliver is removing the gap between what the system THINKS its edge is and what its edge ACTUALLY is -- every finding across 9.38/9.39/this thesis is a case where the system's self-reported performance (win rate, RR, growth rate, go-live gate) was measuring something subtly different from what it believed it was measuring (fabricated R-multiples, flat ghost R-multiples, a Bayesian belief that wasn't Bayesian, a save/load format silently dropping calibration). 'Exponentially better' in this context concretely means: the CONFIDENCE the system has in its own numbers becomes trustworthy, which is what actually lets you size positions correctly (half-Kelly is only correct if p and R are right), decide when to trust a regime read (only meaningful if belief genuinely reflects accumulated evidence), and know whether Phase 6 passing means what it claims (only true if the ghost trades it's built on reflect real TP/SL behavior). This is a multiplier on everything downstream, not a local improvement -- which is the actual justification for calling it 'exponential' rather than incremental: fixing the measurement layer improves the quality of every decision that depends on those measurements, simultaneously.

What's realistic in one build pass: closing the specific, now-identified measurement gaps (ghost R-multiple realism, higher-timeframe regime as an explicit signal rather than a side-channel, currency-strength signal earning IC-based trust like the other 6 components, unifying the two different growth-rate/edge-projection formulas into one). What requires live data to validate: whether any of this actually changes trading outcomes. I can make the system's self-assessment honest; I cannot make live markets cooperate. That has to be said plainly rather than implied away.

=== SECTION 5: PRIORITISED IMPLEMENTATION PLAN ===

Ordered by impact (highest first), not file position:

1. Ghost R-multiple realism (Phase 5/6): replace RecordGhostTrade()'s flat 2.5/-1.5 R-multiples with the trade's actual TP/SL distance ratio, same principle as the 9.39 live-trade fix. Highest priority because Phase 6 is the go-live gate -- if it's built on inaccurate simulated RR, everything downstream inherits that inaccuracy before a single real trade happens.

2. Unify the growth-rate/edge-projection logic: CalculateDefensibleDailyGrowthRate() (9.38, half-Kelly geometric) and the existing 'GAME PLAN' projection inside RunMarketCalibration() (additive edge-per-trade x frequency x risk%) currently coexist as two different formulas answering the same question. Pick one (the half-Kelly geometric version is the more rigorous of the two) and have both call sites use it, so the system doesn't tell the user two different numbers for the same concept.

3. Higher-timeframe regime as an explicit Phase 4 dimension: cheap to compute (reuses the existing hysteresis classifier on H1/H4 data), currently entirely absent from calibration despite being exactly the kind of 'different information location' Section 1 identifies as missing.

4. Route currency-strength/cross-pair signal through Phase 2's IC process: it currently has a fixed contribution regardless of whether it's actually predictive in a given regime, unlike every other signal component.

5. Resolve the regimeStrategies[9-11] reserved-slots question and the DetectCurrentRegime() correlation-override-doesn't-sync-back question (both flagged, not fixed, in 9.38/9.39) -- lower priority than 1-4 since they're each a single, contained decision rather than a systemic pattern, but they're still open.

I'd suggest treating items 1-2 as the 9.40 core (they directly address 'is the system's self-assessment trustworthy', which is the thesis of this whole document) and items 3-5 as a stretch goal if time allows -- rather than trying to do all five in one pass and risk the same 'edited without checking what else depends on it' failure mode Section 3 just diagnosed. Want me to proceed on that basis (1-2 as the committed scope, 3-5 opportunistic), or a different cut?
